answers: JavaScript is THE language of the web. It runs in browsers, powers frontend (React, Vue) and backend (Node.js). Every web developer needs it.
JS basics: variables (let, const), functions, arrays, objects, DOM manipulation, async/await, and fetch API. Master these and you're solid!
Learn JavaScript: freeCodeCamp (free), JavaScript.info (best docs), Traversy Media (YouTube), or The Odin Project for full-stack learning.
JavaScript can build anything: websites, mobile apps (React Native), desktop apps (Electron), servers (Node.js), and even games. Super versatile!

feelings.question: how to learn react and javascript?
feelings.answer: To learn React and JavaScript, you can start by learning HTML/CSS/JavaScript (HTML first, then React docs) and build a simple todo app using APIs like Google Maps or Flickr. Then move on to building full-stack applications with React and React Native for mobile development. Finally, you can dive into advanced topics such as Redux, GraphQL, and React Router. Remember to practice and learn by doing, and don't forget to seek help from online resources like freeCodeCamp, JavaScripT.info, Traversy Media, or The Odin Project for full-stack learning.
